steps.title: Understand the shift transformation
steps.content:
The equation $g(x+28) = \frac{1}{10}(x-a)^2(x-b)^2(x-c)$ relates two functions. The right side has zeros when $x = a$ (double), $x = b$ (double), or $x = c$ (single). These occur exactly when $x+28$ equals a zero of $g$.

Therefore: if $g$ has a zero at $z$, then $g(x+28) = 0$ when $x + 28 = z$, giving $x = z - 28$.
This means the zeros of $g$ shifted left by 28 units are the zeros of the factorization.
steps.formula: $$\text{If } g(z) = 0 \text{, then } g(x+28) = 0 \text{ when } x = z - 28$$

steps.title: Identify the polynomial's structure from the graph
steps.content:
From the graph of $g(x)$:
  • Crossing at $x \approx -2$: the curve crosses the $x$-axis (simple zero, multiplicity 1)
  • Touching behavior around $x \approx 1$ to $2$: the curve approaches $y = 0$ and bounces (double zero, multiplicity 2)
  • Near $x = 3$: the curve is very close to the $x$-axis, consistent with a double zero touching point
The degree-5 polynomial $(x-a)^2(x-b)^2(x-c)$ requires exactly two touching points (the double zeros $a$ and $b$) and one crossing (the simple zero $c$).

steps.title: Apply the backward-shift formula to the zero near $x = 3$
steps.content:
If $g$ has a double zero at $x = 3$, then $g(x+28) = 0$ when $x + 28 = 3$.
Solving: $x = 3 - 28 = -25$.

Since this zero is a double zero of $g$ (touching point), it must correspond to one of the two double zeros of $g(x+28)$, which are the values $a$ and $b$.
Therefore, $b = -25$ (or $a = -25$). That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Verify by eliminating other choices
steps.content:
For each other choice, the corresponding zero of $g$ would lie far outside the visible graph range:
  • Choice A ($b = 31$): requires $g$ to have a zero at $x = 31 + 28 = 59$ — not visible
  • Choice B ($b = 3$): requires $g$ to have a double zero at $x = 3 + 28 = 31$ — not visible
  • Choice C ($b = -2$): would place a double zero at $x = -2 + 28 = 26$ — not visible, and conflicts with the simple zero observed at $x \approx -2$
Only choice D ($b = -25$) corresponds to a zero visible on the graph.
